concerns.text: Task T016 (UCI downloader) and T020 (Population mean calculator) are listed as parallel-safe [P] or independent, but T020 strictly depends on T016 completing the download and T017/T018 completing the cleaning. T020 cannot compute the 'mean of the full UCI dataset array' until the data exists. The current ordering implies T020 could run before T016, violating the producer-consumer dependency.

Task T021 (Sampling logic) is marked [P] (parallel-safe), but it depends on the output of T020 (Population mean) to define the ground truth for the simulation loop, and T018 (Data cleaner) to provide the clean dataset array. Marking T021 as [P] suggests it can run independently of T018/T020, which is semantically incorrect as it consumes their artifacts.

Task T024 (Coverage check logic) is marked [P], but it consumes the 'interval bounds' from T022/T023 and the 'ground truth mean' from T020. It cannot execute until T020, T022, and T023 are complete. The [P] tag is invalid here as it implies no ordering dependency on the interval calculation tasks.

Task T025 (Main Monte Carlo loop) is the consumer of T021 (Sampling), T022 (t-interval), T023 (Bootstrap), and T024 (Coverage check). It is listed as [P] in the text description but logically must be the final step in the US1 chain, consuming all previous outputs. The [P] tag is misleading and incorrect for the main orchestration loop.

Task T029 (Aggregation logic) is marked [P] but depends on the output of T028 (Raw coverage records). T028 is the producer of the data T029 consumes. T029 cannot run in parallel with T028; it must strictly follow it. The [P] tag violates the producer-consumer rule.

Task T035 (Integrate sweep into simulation loop) depends on the completion of the US1 engine (T025) and the specific sweep logic (T034). It is marked [P] but is a dependency consumer of the core simulation engine. It cannot be parallelized with the engine implementation.
Task T039 (Integrate sample size sweep) similarly depends on the US1 engine (T025) and T038. Marking it [P] implies it can run before the engine is ready, which is a semantic ordering violation.

Tasks T005, T006, T007 are marked [P] for creating skeletons. While creating empty files is parallel-safe, the descriptions imply 'skeleton with Monte Carlo loop structure' (T005) which might require knowledge of the data model (T012). If T012 is not complete, T005's structure is undefined. However, as 'skeletons', this is a minor nit compared to the data-flow violations above.
